task_completion.visual_evidence: The CoppeliaSim GUI should be visible on the screen, showing the 3D scene viewer.

task_completion.description: Program the robot arm to trace a circular path at multiple speeds
task_completion.visual_evidence: A script (either inside CoppeliaSim or an external Python script) should be visible containing trigonometric functions (e.g., sin, cos) to generate a circular path, inverse kinematics/Jacobian control logic, and variables defining at least three different speeds or durations.

task_completion.description: Run the simulation to execute the path-tracking at progressively faster speeds
task_completion.visual_evidence: The CoppeliaSim simulation should be actively running (Play button pressed, simulation time advancing) with the robot arm moving, or terminal logs should show the progress of multiple simulation runs at different speeds.

task_completion.description: Save the tracking data to contouring_data.csv
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file named 'contouring_data.csv' must be visible in '/home/ga/Documents/CoppeliaSim/exports/'. If opened or printed, it must contain the headers: speed_profile_id, sim_time_s, target_x, target_y, actual_x, actual_y, radial_error_mm.

task_completion.description: Save the profiling report to contouring_report.json
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file named 'contouring_report.json' must be visible in '/home/ga/Documents/CoppeliaSim/exports/'. If opened or printed, it must contain the fields: total_runs, speeds_tested, max_error_slowest_run_mm, max_error_fastest_run_mm, and physics_lag_detected.

integrity.description: The agent must interact with the actual CoppeliaSim physics engine rather than mocking the data mathematically.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for CoppeliaSim API calls (e.g., ZeroMQ Remote API, sim.getObject, sim.setJointTargetVelocity) in the code, and ensure the CoppeliaSim application was actually run.

integrity.description: The generated data should reflect genuine physics simulation errors, not perfectly mocked data.
integrity.visual_evidence: If the CSV or JSON contents are visible, the radial_error_mm should contain realistic floating-point numbers greater than 0, and max_error_fastest_run_mm should be demonstrably larger than max_error_slowest_run_mm.

integrity.description: The JSON report must be generated programmatically based on the simulation data, not manually typed.
integrity.visual_evidence: The JSON file should be created by a script (e.g., using Python's json.dump) rather than being manually typed out in a text editor with fabricated values.
